title: Revenge Is A Dish Best Served Three Time

jokes.text: Which of the 25 easy-listening stations should we listen to? I say 'Cool Waves.' 'Neon Breeze!' 'Chill Thunder!' 'Chill Thunder!' You're all idiots. I'm putting it on 'Chill Thunder Lite.'
jokes.setup: Family discussing satellite radio station choices
jokes.punchline: The absurd proliferation of nearly identical easy-listening stations with ridiculous names, ending with the compromise of 'Chill Thunder Lite'
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of satellite radio's over-segmentation with escalating absurd station names. Homer's dismissive response and compromise solution feels very in-character.

jokes.text: Then what's America doing in Iraq?
jokes.setup: Marge says revenge never solves anything
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent but pointed political observation
jokes.explanation: Sharp political commentary delivered with perfect economy through a child's innocent logic. Very much in The Simpsons' tradition of subversive humor.

jokes.text: Texas? No, France.
jokes.setup: Marge mentions Paris
jokes.punchline: Homer assumes Paris, Texas - classic Homer ignorance
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer stupidity gag - well-executed but predictable character beat.

jokes.text: The way all our words are either a girl or a guy. Oh, that's the best.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to love France
jokes.punchline: His crude understanding of grammatical gender
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike fascination with grammatical gender shows his shallow understanding while being genuinely endearing.

jokes.text: Don't worry! They're still drunk from school.
jokes.setup: Homer worried about kids seeing him arrested
jokes.punchline: French schoolchildren being drunk is presented as normal
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny stereotype about French culture delivered with perfect casualness. The economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Uh... deux... trois.
jokes.setup: French children stumbling drunkenly
jokes.punchline: They can barely count to three in French while intoxicated
jokes.explanation: Visual payoff to the drunk schoolchildren gag - simple but effective followthrough.

jokes.text: Even if I did love England, I'd play it cool. Wait for it to come to me.
jokes.setup: Homer defending against treason charges
jokes.punchline: Applying dating advice to international relations
jokes.explanation: Absurd application of romantic strategy to geopolitics. Perfect Homer logic that treats countries like potential romantic partners.

jokes.text: You moron! I'm already alive. In prison.
jokes.setup: Judge sentences Homer to life
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation and delayed realization
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer literal-mindedness followed by dim realization. Well-structured with good character integration.

jokes.text: Hey, wait, did you guys hear that? He just admitted it. Guys? Oh, man, this place is so unfair.
jokes.setup: Man in iron mask admits to being the rightful king
jokes.punchline: Homer tries to snitch but no one cares about his obvious observation
jokes.explanation: Homer's social obliviousness and whining about unfairness creates mild cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Well, that and the rat helmet.
jokes.setup: Not knowing is the worst torture of all
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of actual horrific torture device
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect casualness. The contrast between psychological and physical torture is expertly handled.

jokes.text: Can you loosen my arms so I can shake my fists? You're the traitor. Revenge! Okay. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to dramatically threaten revenge
jokes.punchline: He politely asks for help with the gesture and thanks the guard
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of dramatic revenge declaration through Homer's politeness. The structure builds perfectly to the absurd 'thank you.'

jokes.text: Oh, sorry, I wasn't listening. I was checking out my reflection in this yellow water. Ho-ho-ho-ho, looking good.
jokes.setup: Cellmate explains Homer is self-centered
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately proves it by admiring himself in prison toilet water
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Homer's vanity overriding even basic hygiene. The immediate proof of the accusation is expertly timed.

jokes.text: Also, because while you slept, I violated you repeatedly.
jokes.setup: Old man's touching speech about wanting one friend before he dies
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals prison sexual assault
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that relies on shock value more than craft. The misdirection works but the payoff feels cheap and potentially offensive.

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer falling through tunnel
jokes.punchline: Classic Homer exclamation after mishap
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer catchphrase - not original but perfectly deployed and economical.

jokes.text: Ew! Silver. Ooh, a penny. This is my lucky day!
jokes.setup: Homer finding buried treasure
jokes.punchline: He's disappointed by silver but thrilled by a penny
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - dismissing valuable silver for worthless copper. Shows his backwards priorities beautifully.

jokes.text: Oh, I love today's music. Frère this, and Jacques that. Out of sight.
jokes.setup: Period-appropriate French folk song playing
jokes.punchline: Marge treating centuries-old song as contemporary hit with '60s slang
jokes.explanation: Clever anachronism joke mixing time periods. Marge's enthusiasm for 'modern' music that's actually ancient is well-crafted.

jokes.text: They're good for doing the wife, right?
jokes.setup: Discussion of massage chair
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual innuendo about marital relations
jokes.explanation: Low-brow sexual humor that doesn't add much to the story. Feels like cheap laugh attempt.

jokes.text: Okay, lesson learned. Friends again?
jokes.setup: Homer has just killed Meaux with elaborate revenge contraption
jokes.punchline: Meaux casually suggests they make up as he's dying
jokes.explanation: Excellent understatement - treating murder as minor disagreement. The casualness makes it darkly funny.

jokes.text: We were together for five years. Now who's going to take care of the triplets?
jokes.setup: Homer killed Marge's husband for her
jokes.punchline: She actually loved the guy and they have children together
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing Homer's revenge was completely misguided. The triplets detail makes it worse.

jokes.text: Plus, in the middle, I had to take revenge on some of my contractors.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why his revenge took so long
jokes.punchline: He got sidetracked taking revenge on people helping him get revenge
jokes.explanation: Brilliant recursive absurdity - revenge within revenge. Very Homer-like logic and well-crafted escalation.

jokes.text: I'm gonna get my revenge, and if I get caught, I'm pinning it on the baby like the time I shot Mr. Burns.
jokes.setup: Homer still wants revenge despite the story
jokes.punchline: Reference to iconic 'Who Shot Mr. Burns?' storyline
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to famous storyline, though relying heavily on nostalgia rather than new humor.

jokes.text: This is the turning point in the eternal battle of brain v. brawn.
jokes.setup: Martin showing off revenge device
jokes.punchline: His overly dramatic description of a simple revenge plan
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Martin's pretentiousness, though not particularly original.

jokes.text: I can handle the glove. I took juggling at camp.
jokes.setup: Milhouse volunteering to operate revenge device
jokes.punchline: His irrelevant qualification of summer camp juggling
jokes.explanation: Typical Milhouse non-sequitur connecting unrelated skills. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Oh, you are so dead you're alive.
jokes.setup: Jimbo threatening the nerds
jokes.punchline: His confused attempt at a tough-guy threat
jokes.explanation: Jimbo's dim-witted attempt at intimidation creates a logical paradox that's funnier for being unintentional.

jokes.text: My fun bags! Please, have mercy! I always thought you were the coolest dork.
jokes.setup: Bullies being attacked by revenge device
jokes.punchline: Tough bully calling testicles 'fun bags' and begging for mercy
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor about anatomy with role reversal. The 'coolest dork' line adds some character depth.

jokes.text: Except the ones with asthma, which is all of them.
jokes.setup: The nerds can breathe easy
jokes.punchline: They all have asthma anyway
jokes.explanation: Relies on stereotype about nerdy kids but delivers it with good structure and economy.

jokes.text: Yup, having a weapon at school has really made things awesome.
jokes.setup: Bart praising Milhouse's success with revenge
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to bringing weapons to school as positive
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about school violence delivered with typical Simpsons casualness about serious issues.

jokes.text: That's the only line I get in your stupid story?!
jokes.setup: Bart says his catchphrase 'Ay caramba'
jokes.punchline: He complains about his limited role in Lisa's story
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor breaking the fourth wall to comment on storytelling conventions. Well-integrated with character dynamics.

jokes.text: There are no small parts, just small actors.
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about his small role
jokes.punchline: Lisa's theatrical cliché with implied insult about Bart's height
jokes.explanation: Lisa's intellectual response that doubles as a subtle dig. The theatre reference fits her character well.

jokes.text: This is my first day at this school. I haven't done anything to you! You will. This is prevenge.
jokes.setup: New student pleading innocence
jokes.punchline: Milhouse invented 'prevenge' for future crimes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay with 'prevenge' - preventive revenge. Dark concept delivered with perfect Milhouse logic. Highly quotable neologism.

jokes.text: You will. This is prevenge.
jokes.setup: New kid says he hasn't done anything to Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's made-up word for preemptive revenge
jokes.explanation: Excellent made-up word that's both logical and absurd. Shows Milhouse's descent into revenge madness with perfect economy.

jokes.text: Opening the cup on your weirdo pills?
jokes.setup: Jimbo asking what the power glove is for
jokes.punchline: His creative insult about Milhouse needing medication
jokes.explanation: Decent bully insult with some creativity. The 'weirdo pills' phrasing has character-appropriate crudeness.

jokes.text: No, sweetie, the lesson is: never put down your weapon.
jokes.setup: Lisa explains moral lesson about revenge making you as bad as your enemy
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misses the point and draws violent conclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - taking exactly the wrong lesson from a moral story. The misdirection is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: You're too dumb to tell a story. No, seriously, you're great. Let's hear it.
jokes.setup: Homer dismissing Bart's storytelling ability
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction when he realizes he was rude
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's lack of filter followed by awkward attempt at recovery.

jokes.text: My story takes place in a time I call the past.
jokes.setup: Bart beginning his story with dramatic flair
jokes.punchline: His overly obvious description of when stories take place
jokes.explanation: Bart's pseudo-intellectual storytelling style paired with stating the obvious creates good humor.

jokes.text: Avenge me. In flamboyant, impractical fashion.
jokes.setup: Homer's dying words
jokes.punchline: Specific request for style of revenge
jokes.explanation: Excellent parody of dramatic death scenes. Homer's specific aesthetic requirements for his revenge are perfectly in character.

jokes.text: In my younger days, I was an ace crime fighter. The 'Crimson Cockatoo.'
jokes.setup: Grandpa revealing superhero past
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous bird-themed name
jokes.explanation: Good Grandpa delusion with absurd superhero name. Cockatoo is an unexpected bird choice for a hero.

jokes.text: Holy Hannah! I've got more muscles than a New England clam bank.
jokes.setup: Bart finishing superhero training montage
jokes.punchline: Old-fashioned exclamation with regional seafood reference
jokes.explanation: Creative period-appropriate exclamation with unusual comparison. The New England clam bank reference is nicely specific.

jokes.text: You can be the Crimson Cockaboy. Hmm? I'll keep thinking.
jokes.setup: Grandpa suggesting superhero name based on his own
jokes.punchline: The unfortunate sexual implications of 'Cockaboy'
jokes.explanation: Juvenile wordplay humor. The immediate recognition and backtracking adds some charm.

jokes.text: Your overzealous homicide has saved me 80 cents.
jokes.setup: Bart-Man saves newspapers from thief
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to murder for minor theft
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor treating vigilante murder as minor business transaction. The casual tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: like Howard Hughes knocks up young starlets!
jokes.setup: 1940s radio describing Bart-Man's crime fighting
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual reference in family-friendly radio show
jokes.explanation: Anachronistic humor about 1940s radio being surprisingly crude. Historical reference works but isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: But I'm not a villain. I'm a transvestite. Explain that to your maker.
jokes.setup: Bart threatens villain
jokes.punchline: Person claims innocence as transvestite before Bart attacks anyway
jokes.explanation: Problematic humor that treats cross-dressing as inherently suspicious while also showing Bart as unreasonably violent.

jokes.text: Hope that's cobracetic. Hey, jerk, puns are lazy writing. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Villain making snake puns
jokes.punchline: Homer breaking character to criticize pun-based humor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor with Homer breaking character to critique the writing. Self-aware comedy at its best, followed by his own catchphrase.

jokes.text: No kidding because you're standing in the dip.
jokes.setup: Bart-Man declares party's over
jokes.punchline: He's literally standing in party dip at fancy gathering
jokes.explanation: Visual gag playing on 'party's over' cliché. Simple but effective physical comedy.

jokes.text: Plus, I got zillions of dollars and no parents to tell me what to do.
jokes.setup: Question about whether revenge was worth it
jokes.punchline: Bart focusing on the financial benefits of being an orphan
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about orphanhood benefits delivered with Bart's typical self-interest. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Don't talk to me when I'm vibrating.
jokes.setup: Alfred trying to talk to Bart-Man
jokes.punchline: Bart's bizarre request about not being disturbed while vibrating
jokes.explanation: Weird non-sequitur that's funny for its complete randomness and delivery. Very quotable absurdist humor.

jokes.text: We're both from Connecticut.
jokes.setup: Homer says he and the other driver have a lot in common
jokes.punchline: The most mundane possible commonality
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimactic resolution. The mundanity of sharing a home state as major bonding point is very Homer.

jokes.text: Two? You think there are two... Well, I never. How could you... Why do you think it is that mankind is so inclined towards hating one another?
jokes.setup: Driver outraged by Homer's star count estimate
jokes.punchline: Sudden shift to philosophical question about human nature
jokes.explanation: Funny misdirection from anger about astronomical ignorance to deep philosophical inquiry. The non-sequitur works well.
